question: How to embed a video in HTML
description: Use the <video> element to embed self-hosted videos with multiple format fallbacks, poster images, and performance-friendly preload settings.

steps.title: Basic video with controls
steps.explanation: The controls attribute renders the browser's native play/pause/volume UI. Always provide alt-equivalent content inside <video> for non-supporting browsers.

steps.title: Add a poster image
steps.explanation: The poster attribute sets a thumbnail shown before the video plays. This prevents an ugly blank frame on slow connections.
steps.code:
<video
  width="1280"
  height="720"
  controls
  poster="thumbnail.jpg"
  preload="metadata"
>
  <source src="video.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
  <source src="video.webm" type="video/webm" />
</video>

steps.title: Auto-playing background video
steps.explanation: For decorative background videos, use autoplay muted playsinline loop. muted is required for autoplay to work in most browsers. playsinline prevents fullscreen on iOS.
steps.code:
<video
  autoplay
  muted
  loop
  playsinline
  preload="auto"
  aria-hidden="true"
>
  <source src="bg-video.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
  <source src="bg-video.webm" type="video/webm" />
</video>

steps.title: Control preload behaviour
steps.explanation: Use preload to balance performance. 'none' downloads nothing until play — best for many videos per page. 'metadata' downloads only duration/dimensions. 'auto' downloads the whole file.
steps.code:
<!-- Many videos on one page: don't preload -->
<video preload="none" poster="thumb.jpg" controls>
  <source src="video.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
</video>

<!-- Single hero video: preload metadata -->
<video preload="metadata" poster="hero-thumb.jpg" controls>
  <source src="hero.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
</video>
